mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll employment growth decelerates below 100,000 net new jobs, signaling weakening labor demand and reduced consumer spending capacity, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 100,000 persons
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite weak payroll signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in real retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals deteriorating aggregate demand and consumer confidence, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month simultaneously
regime_hint: demand-side recession or sharp demand contraction
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month, signaling expanding productive capacity and improving supply-side conditions that support corporate profit margins and produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO acceleration are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rises above 3.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 2.0%, creating a negative real interest rate that incentivizes equity allocation over cash and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient reverses sign or becomes insignificant (p > 0.15) in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate rises above 5.0%, signaling labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: recession or sharp labor market deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite elevated unemployment, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 1.5% annualized rate, signaling weakening economic momentum and reduced corporate revenue growth expectations, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below 1.5%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above 1,600 thousand units annualized while real retail sales (RSAFS) grow above 2% month-over-month, signaling robust consumer demand and construction activity that supports corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: expansion phase with strong consumer demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient becomes insignificant (p > 0.15) in rolling logistic regressions over 3-year windows.
